summary:
# Summary

The provided source text is a structured summary and outline for a live-streamed gaming session titled "Summer Break!" hosted by @gnomedic as part of the GameRT initiative. The content functions primarily as a record of the stream's agenda rather than an empirical study or theoretical article. Consequently, it lacks traditional research components such as hypotheses, data collection methods, or statistical analysis. Instead, it documents the procedural flow of a Tabletop Role-Playing Game (TTRPG) session designed for a library audience.

The primary purpose of the text is to outline the three-part structure of the broadcast: an introduction to the game rules, an actual playthrough, and a post-game discussion. The game featured, *Summer Break!*, is identified as a micro-RPG found in *The Ultimate Micro-RPG Book* edited by James D’amato. The stream begins with a technical check and an introduction to the hosts, Russell and Danielle, who guide the audience through character creation, fort creation, and danger creation mechanics. This segment serves to educate viewers on the specific rules of the micro-RPG format, which is characterized by its brevity and simplicity compared to traditional TTRPGs.

The second phase involves the actual gameplay, described in the text as "Fun Stuff." While no specific narrative outcomes are detailed, this section represents the practical application of the game mechanics in a live setting. The third and most significant section for library service implications is the discussion phase. Here, the hosts address several key themes relevant to Games-Based Library Services (GBLS). First, they discuss "Figuring out Rules & Playtesting," indicating an interest in the iterative nature of game design and adaptation. Second, they explore "Playing at different life stages" and "What childhood means to different people." This suggests that the session aimed to leverage the nostalgic themes of *Summer Break!* to facilitate intergenerational or reflective dialogue among adult participants, a common goal in community library programming.

A critical component of the discussion is "Programing Potential," which directly addresses the utility of such games in library settings. The text implies that micro-RPGs offer accessible entry points for patrons due to their low barrier to entry and short duration. Furthermore, the session explicitly incorporates "Safety Tools Considerations." The source text lists several specific resources, including "Script Change" by Beau Jágr Sheldon, "The Door is Open" by Eirik Fatland, and the "TTRPG Safety Toolkit" collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk. This inclusion underscores a commitment to creating inclusive and safe gaming environments, a best practice in modern library programming. The text also provides a list of "Play Alikes," such as *Sleepaway*, *Camp Flying Moose For Girls of All Kinds*, and *Kids on Bikes*, offering curatorial recommendations for similar thematic experiences.

The document concludes with promotional information for future GameRT events, specifically a "Round Table" discussion scheduled for September 9th. It also reiterates the Twitch policy emphasizing kindness, respect, and zero tolerance for hate speech, reinforcing the safety framework mentioned earlier. Overall, while the text does not present empirical data, it provides a clear example of how libraries can structure TTRPG programming to include educational components, thematic discussion, and robust safety protocols. It highlights the intersection of nostalgia, game mechanics, and community engagement in library services.
sourceText:
What are we Playing?
Summer Break! by @gnomedic
Watch the VOD of this session here: https://www.twitch.tv/collections/tpuBAP2vGhcF7A
Gnomedic’s Pages https://twitter.com/gnomedic?lang=en https://www.twitch.tv/gnomedic https://gnomedic.itch.io/ https://gnomedic.wixsite.com/portfolio
Found in: The Ultimate Micro-RPG Book edited by James D’amato https://www.simonandschuster.com/books/The-Ultimate-Micro-RPG-Book/James-D-Amato/TheUltimate-RPG-Guide-Series/9781507212868
James D’amato’s Pages https://twitter.com/OneShotRPG http://oneshotpodcast.com/
Play Alikes
Summer & Nostalgia Vibes ● Sleepaway : Possum Creek Games ● Camp Flying Moose For Girls of All Kinds: Alicia Furness ● Two Summers: Côme Martin ● An Altogether Different River: Aaron Lim
Kid at Heart Vibes ● Crescent Moon: Pocket Compass Co. ● BFF! Best Friends Forever: Heart of the Deernicorn ● The Tragedies of Middle School: 9th Level Games ● Kids on Bikes: Renegade Game Studios
Fort Vibes ● Fort: Leder Games
Extra Resources ● Safety Tools ○ Script Change by Beau Jágr Sheldon ○ The Door is Open by Eirik Fatland ■ Blog by Lizzie Stark ■ Blog 2 by Eirik Fatland ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk

■ TTRPG Safety Toolkit Guide ■ Google Drive
Twitch Policy
1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GameRT Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Questions? Email gamert@ala.org
Stream Summary
Tech Check on Twitch then Go Time!
Part 1: Intro and Rules ● Who are we & what are we doing??? ● Breakdown of Stream ● Breakdown of Game ○ Character Creation ○ Fort Creation ○ Danger Creation ● Segue: Ready to play?
Part 2: Actual Play ● Fun Stuff ● Segue: How do you Feel?
Part 3: Discussion ● Figuring out Rules & Playtesting ● Playing at different life stages ○ What childhood means to different people ● Programing Potential ● Safety Tools Considerations ● Chat Questions & Comments ● Segue: That’s all we’ve got for this session…

Ending ● Come back again on September 9th for our newest twitch program the Round Table round table, where we’ve gathered our nerdiest librarians to talk about all things games ● Thank you so much for joinings for ● Russell: Low Stress and ● Danielle: High Mayhem ● Bye!!
